title: Grid: Row, Column & Container
intro: Three flex primitives carry every wireframe layout: `wf-row` lays children out horizontally, `wf-column` stacks them vertically, and `wf-container` constrains and centers a page's width. Spacing between children always comes from the `wf-gap-*` scale on the 8px grid — never an inline literal.

blocks.body:
`wf-row` and `wf-column` are the same flexbox primitive with the axis swapped — `wf-row` sets `flex-direction: row`, `wf-column` sets `flex-direction: column`. Everything else (gaps, justify, align) is shared utility classes, so once you know one you know both. Reach for them anywhere you'd otherwise reach for a bare `display: flex` div; in production they map to Excalibrr's `<Horizontal>` and `<Vertical>`.

The one axis gotcha to internalize: `wf-justify-*` is the main axis and `wf-align-*` is the cross axis, so they flip meaning between the two. In a `wf-row`, `wf-justify-*` distributes horizontally and `wf-align-*` aligns vertically; in a `wf-column` it's the reverse. Same classes, swapped result.

`wf-container` is structural, not decorative — it's a `width: 100%` wrapper with auto side margins and horizontal padding whose only job is to cap and center content width on wide screens. It carries no border, background, or shadow on its own. When you want a card-like surface, that's `wf-container-elevated` (or `wf-card`), not a plain container.

blocks.title: Row with gap
blocks.caption: `wf-row` arranges children horizontally; `wf-gap-*` adds the 8px-grid spacing between them. Shown: wf-gap-2, wf-gap-6, and wf-gap-10.

blocks.title: Row justify-content
blocks.caption: Main-axis distribution on a row: wf-justify-start (default), -center, -end, -between, and -around.

blocks.title: Row & column structure
blocks.intro: The two direction primitives. Compose them freely — a column of rows is the backbone of most page layouts.

rows.name: wf-row
rows.when: Lay children out horizontally (display: flex; flex-direction: row). Maps to Excalibrr <Horizontal>.

rows.name: wf-column
rows.when: Stack children vertically (display: flex; flex-direction: column). Maps to Excalibrr <Vertical>.

blocks.title: Gap scale
blocks.intro: Spacing between children comes from `wf-gap-*` only — never an inline margin or padding literal. Each step resolves to a `--wf-space-*` token on the 8px grid. The base steps (wf-gap-1 through wf-gap-8) live in the token utilities; layout.css adds the two large steps below for page-level spacing.

rows.name: wf-gap-2
rows.when: Tight spacing between closely related items — toolbar buttons, chips.

rows.name: wf-gap-4
rows.when: The workhorse default for most rows and stacks (gap: var(--wf-space-4)).

rows.name: wf-gap-6
rows.when: Looser separation between distinct groups in a row, e.g. nav links.

rows.name: wf-gap-10
rows.when: Large layout spacing (gap: var(--wf-space-10)). Defined in layout.css, not the base utilities.

rows.name: wf-gap-12
rows.when: Largest layout spacing (gap: var(--wf-space-12)). Defined in layout.css.

blocks.title: Justify (main axis)
blocks.intro: Distribution along the flex direction — horizontal in a row, vertical in a column. wf-justify-start is the implicit default.

rows.name: wf-justify-start
rows.when: Pack children to the start (justify-content: flex-start). The default — usually omit it.

rows.name: wf-justify-center
rows.when: Center the group on the main axis (justify-content: center).

rows.name: wf-justify-end
rows.when: Pack children to the end (justify-content: flex-end). Right-aligned actions.

rows.name: wf-justify-between
rows.when: First/last to the edges, equal space between (justify-content: space-between). The header pattern: logo left, actions right.

rows.name: wf-justify-around
rows.when: Equal space around each child (justify-content: space-around).

rows.name: wf-justify-evenly
rows.when: Equal space between and at the ends (justify-content: space-evenly).

blocks.title: Column with gap
blocks.caption: `wf-column` stacks children vertically with the same `wf-gap-*` scale. Shown: wf-gap-2, wf-gap-4, wf-gap-6.

blocks.title: Align (cross axis)
blocks.intro: Alignment across the flex direction — vertical in a row, horizontal in a column. `wf-align-baseline` is row-only (it's scoped to `.wf-row` in the CSS).

rows.name: wf-align-start
rows.when: Align children to the cross-axis start (align-items: flex-start).

rows.name: wf-align-center
rows.when: Center on the cross axis (align-items: center). The most common pairing with wf-justify-between in headers.

rows.name: wf-align-end
rows.when: Align to the cross-axis end (align-items: flex-end).

rows.name: wf-align-stretch
rows.when: Stretch children to fill the cross axis (align-items: stretch). Equal-width cards in a column, equal-height in a row.

rows.name: wf-align-baseline
rows.when: Align children to their text baseline (align-items: baseline). Row only.

blocks.title: Wrap & flex-item utilities
blocks.intro: Control overflow on a row and how individual children grow or shrink within either primitive.

rows.name: wf-wrap
rows.when: Let row children wrap to the next line when they exceed the container (flex-wrap: wrap). Row only.

rows.name: wf-nowrap
rows.when: Force a single line (flex-wrap: nowrap). Row only.

rows.name: wf-flex-1
rows.when: Make a child take all remaining space (flex: 1 1 0%). The growing main region in a header/content/footer column.

rows.name: wf-grow
rows.when: Grow to fill without zeroing the basis (flex-grow: 1).

rows.name: wf-shrink-0
rows.when: Stop a child from shrinking — fixed-width sidebars, icons (flex-shrink: 0).

blocks.title: Container size variants
blocks.caption: `wf-container` max-width modifiers center and cap page content: sm (640px), md (768px), lg (1024px), xl (1280px).

blocks.title: Container width & padding
blocks.intro: `wf-container` alone is full-width with 16px side padding and auto margins. Add a size modifier to cap the width, or a padding modifier to change the side gutter.

rows.name: wf-container
rows.when: Base wrapper: width 100%, auto side margins, 16px (--wf-space-4) horizontal padding. No border or background.

rows.name: wf-container-sm
rows.when: Cap content at max-width: 640px and center it.

rows.name: wf-container-md
rows.when: Cap at max-width: 768px. Comfortable reading width for forms and prose.

rows.name: wf-container-lg
rows.when: Cap at max-width: 1024px. The default app content width.

rows.name: wf-container-xl
rows.when: Cap at max-width: 1280px for wide dashboards.

rows.name: wf-container-2xl
rows.when: Cap at max-width: 1536px — the widest constraint.

rows.name: wf-container-full
rows.when: max-width: 100% — explicitly opt out of any width cap.

rows.name: wf-container-tight
rows.when: Reduce side padding to 8px (--wf-space-2).

rows.name: wf-container-relaxed
rows.when: Widen side padding to 24px (--wf-space-6).

rows.name: wf-container-spacious
rows.when: Widen side padding to 32px (--wf-space-8).

rows.name: wf-container-elevated
rows.when: Card-like surface: surface background, border, radius, and shadow. Use when you want a container that also reads as a card.

blocks.title: Header layout — row composition
blocks.code:
Logo
Home About Contact
blocks.note: wf-justify-between pushes the three groups to edges and center; wf-align-center vertically centers them. Nested rows carry their own gaps.

blocks.title: App shell — container + sidebar + main
blocks.note: Container caps and centers the page; wf-shrink-0 holds the sidebar width while wf-flex-1 lets main absorb the rest.

items.rule: All spacing between elements comes from the spacing scale via wf-gap-* — never a raw px gap.
items.why: There are no 7px gaps, 10px paddings, or 18px margins. Every wf-gap-* resolves to a --wf-space-* token that sits on the 8px grid.

items.rule: When the gap you want isn't on the scale, step one up or one down — don't invent a custom value.
items.why: If the scale doesn't have a token at the size you want, that's the signal to pick the nearest step, not to write a literal (DESIGN.md §5.2 + §7.6).

items.rule: Default vertical rhythm is tight: related rows stack at --wf-space-stack-sm (8px), groups at --wf-space-stack-lg.
items.why: Gravitate is enterprise data-density. A region using 3+ different vertical rhythms is broken (DESIGN.md §5.4).

items.rule: wf-container is for width control only — never use it as a styled box.
items.why: It has no border, padding-beyond-gutter, or background by design. Reach for wf-container-elevated or wf-card when you need a visible surface (DESIGN.md §7.1).
Spacing must come from the wf-gap-* scale on the 8px grid, not an inline literal (DESIGN.md §5.2).

items.do: wf-justify-between for main-axis distribution, wf-align-center for the cross axis
items.why: justify is the main axis and align is the cross axis — the modifiers are not interchangeable, and the wrong-axis class simply doesn't exist in the CSS.
Use the size modifier for width and wf-container-elevated for a surface; a plain container is structural and carries no visual weight (DESIGN.md §7.1).
The class is wf-column (mapping to Excalibrr <Vertical>). wf-col is not defined in layout.css and applies nothing.

items.title: justify and align flip meaning between row and column
items.detail: Both primitives share the same wf-justify-* and wf-align-* classes. In a wf-row, justify is horizontal and align is vertical; in a wf-column it's the reverse. The class name never tells you which screen axis you're moving — the parent's direction does.

items.title: Gap classes are split across two files
items.detail: wf-gap-1 through wf-gap-8 live in the base token utilities; only wf-gap-10 and wf-gap-12 are defined in layout.css. If a wf-gap-* step renders no spacing, confirm the token utilities stylesheet is linked alongside layout.css — both are required.

items.title: Alignment modifiers are scoped to their primitive
items.detail: The CSS writes .wf-row.wf-align-center and .wf-column.wf-align-center as compound selectors, and wf-align-baseline + wf-wrap/wf-nowrap exist only under .wf-row. An align or wrap class on a plain div (no wf-row/wf-column) does nothing.

items.title: wf-container centers via auto margins, not flex
items.detail: It applies margin-left/right: auto with width: 100% — so it centers itself within its parent's width. It only visibly centers once a max-width modifier (wf-container-sm…2xl) caps the width; without one it's full-bleed and there's nothing to center.
